{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_0dbb777b54614808ad667304","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Hardware-Free Runtime Reproduce\n2\t\n3\t**Canonical script:** `scripts/run-sim-local.sh`\n4\t**Verified:** 2026-07-08 — `5bbe52c` (post bf-243os sim→blob path fix), re-verified at HEAD `f859f3e` for bf-40hc acceptance, and final-clean-rebuild re-verified at HEAD `c927f67` (the bf-40hc doc commit itself).\n5\t**Resolves:** the ambiguity that caused bf-40hc's repeated failures; closes the chain bf-3zll → bf-3hji → bf-51fq → bf-5cr3, and **satisfies bf-40hc** (hardware-free runtime path that creates a blob).\n6\t\n7\tThis is the single deterministic artifact that captures the working end-to-end\n8\truntime path — **sim connects → CSI ingested → phase/fusion → tracked blob** — with\n9\tzero hardware, zero Docker, zero manual IP, and zero manual token. Later beads\n10\t(e.g. the blob-identity runtime checks under **bf-f841**) and reruns should start\n11\tfrom here instead of re-deriving the command sequence or the tuning.\n12\t\n13\t---\n14\t\n15\t## TL;DR\n16\t\n17\t```bash\n18\t./scripts/run-sim-local.sh\n19\t# expects, within ~30s:\n20\t#   [run-sim] mothership healthy\n21\t#     t= 1s  nodes_online=4  blobs=2  peak=2\n22\t#     ...\n23\t#   [run-sim] peak_blob_count=2\n24\t#   [run-sim] PASS: >=1 blob via /api/blobs (peak=2) AND sim --verify passed\n25\t```\n26\t\n27\tExit 0 iff ≥1 tracked blob is observed via `GET /api/blobs` over the run window.\n28\t\n29\t---\n30\t\n31\t## What the script does (the exact reproduce sequence)\n32\t\n33\t1. **Build mothership from source** — `cd mothership && go build -o /tmp/spaxel-mothership ./cmd/mothership`\n34\t2. **Build the canonical sim from source** — `cd mothership && go build -o /tmp/spaxel-sim ./cmd/sim`\n35\t3. **Start the mothership** on an ephemeral port + data dir:\n36\t   ```bash\n37\t   SPAXEL_BIND_ADDR=127.0.0.1:8088 \\\n38\t   SPAXEL_DATA_DIR=$(mktemp -d) \\\n39\t   SPAXEL_LOG_LEVEL=warn \\\n40\t   SPAXEL_MDNS_ENABLED=false \\\n41\t   TZ=UTC \\\n42\t     /tmp/spaxel-mothership &\n43\t   ```\n44\t4. **Wait for health** — poll `GET /healthz` until `{\"status\":\"ok\"}`.\n45\t5. **Run the sim** with `--verify`:\n46\t   ```bash\n47\t   /tmp/spaxel-sim \\\n48\t     --mothership ws://localhost:8088/ws/node \\\n49\t     --nodes 4 --walkers 2 --rate 30 --space 5x5x2.5 \\\n50\t     --duration 25 --seed 42 --verify\n51\t   ```\n52\t6. **Observe** — poll `GET /api/blobs` once per second for the whole streaming\n53\t   window, keep the peak, gate on `peak ≥ 1`. (`sim --verify` is a reported\n54\t   secondary cross-check that asserts `blob_count == walkers ±1`.)\n55\t\n56\tEach step is in `scripts/run-sim-local.sh` with a `trap` cleanup that kills both\n57\tprocesses and removes the temp data dir.\n58\t\n59\t---\n60\t\n61\t## The two gotchas (read these before touching the path)\n62\t\n63\t### 1. There are TWO `cmd/sim` packages — only one has `--verify`\n64\t\n65\t| Path | Module | Has `--verify`? | Use it? |\n66\t|------|--------|-----------------|---------|\n67\t| `mothership/cmd/sim/` | part of the mothership module | **YES** (`verify.go`) | **YES — canonical** |\n68\t| `cmd/sim/` | separate, older module (own `go.mod`) | no | **NO** |\n69\t\n70\t`scripts/run-sim-local.sh` builds **only** from `mothership/cmd/sim` and\n71\tadditionally guards against a stale binary by checking `--help` for `--verify`,\n72\trebuilding if missing. **Do not** build/run `cmd/sim` for runtime blob checks.\n73\t\n74\t### 2. `rate=30`, not the upstream default `20`\n75\t\n76\tAt the upstream default `--rate 20`, fusion produces a peak only *while* a walker\n77\tmoves enough to cross the DeltaRMS threshold, so a run can legitimately land\n78\t`peak=2` *or* `peak=0` nondeterministically (see the comment in\n79\t`mothership/cmd/sim/verify.go`). Bumping `--rate` to **30** reliably clears the\n80\tthreshold: every observed run hits `peak == walkers` with the first blob at ~1s.\n81\tThis is a rate/tuning fact, **not** a broken pipeline — if a bead reports 0 blobs,\n82\tfirst re-run at `rate=30` before suspecting a defect.\n83\t\n84\t`--duration 25` (not 40) keeps the sim's late-window `--verify` sample inside the\n85\tactive-walk phase; at long durations walkers drift into low-signal positions and\n86\tverify can spuriously report 0 even though the `/api/blobs` window still saw blobs.\n87\t\n88\t---\n89\t\n90\t## Token path (no manual token needed)\n91\t\n92\tThe canonical sim authenticates exactly like real provisioned firmware:\n93\t\n94\t- `--token` defaults to empty → the sim calls `POST /api/provision` to mint a real\n95\t  per-node HMAC token (`HMAC-SHA256(install_secret, node_mac)`), sent as the\n96\t  `X-Spaxel-Token` header on the WebSocket dial, then bridged into `hello.Token`\n97\t  for validation (bf-1o7qi).\n98\t- Fallback: if provisioning fails, the sim generates a synthetic token. Either\n99\t  way the connection is not left tokenless-by-design.\n100\t- Defense-in-depth: the mothership also tolerates tokenless nodes for\n101\t  `SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS` (default 24h) after startup, so even a token\n102\t  glitch during a reproduce run does not cause a `reject` (bf-2hdbg). The script\n103\t  counts `reject`/`401`/`403` lines in the sim log and reports the count; expect 0.\n104\t\n105\tIf you ever see rejects, check `SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS` (the e2e harness\n106\tsets it to `0` to force strict validation) and that the binary is built from\n107\t`mothership/cmd/sim`.\n108\t\n109\t---\n110\t\n111\t## Environment (mothership)\n112\t\n113\t| Variable | Value in the script | Why |\n114\t|----------|---------------------|-----|\n115\t| `SPAXEL_BIND_ADDR` | `127.0.0.1:8088` | Loopback; avoid clashing with anything on 8080 |\n116\t| `SPAXEL_DATA_DIR` | ephemeral `mktemp -d` | Clean state every run; removed on exit |\n117\t| `SPAXEL_LOG_LEVEL` | `warn` | Quiet enough to scan, loud enough on real problems |\n118\t| `SPAXEL_MDNS_ENABLED` | `false` | Loopback reproduce needs no multicast; sim dials directly |\n119\t| `TZ` | `UTC` | Deterministic diurnal/timestamp behavior |\n120\t\n121\t## Parameters (sim)\n122\t\n123\t| Flag | Default in script | Notes |\n124\t|------|-------------------|-------|\n125\t| `--nodes` | `4` | Enough for stable multi-link fusion |\n126\t| `--walkers` | `2` | `peak == walkers` at rate=30 |\n127\t| `--rate` | `30` | **See gotcha #2** — 20 is nondeterministic |\n128\t| `--duration` | `25` | Keeps `--verify`'s late sample in the active-walk phase |\n129\t| `--seed` | `42` | Reproducible walker paths |\n130\t| `--space` | `5x5x2.5` | Default room geometry |\n131\t| `--verify` | (set) | Canonical acceptance: `blob_count == walkers ±1` + within-2m check |\n132\t\n133\tAll overridable via env: `SIM_NODES`, `SIM_WALKERS`, `SIM_RATE`, `SIM_DURATION`,\n134\t`SIM_SEED`, `SIM_SPACE`, `SIM_PORT`. Binary paths via `SPAXEL_MOTHERSHIP_BIN` /\n135\t`SPAXEL_SIM_BIN`.\n136\t\n137\t---\n138\t\n139\t## Expected result (reference run, HEAD `5bbe52c`)\n140\t\n141\t```\n142\tpeak_blob_count=2\n143\tfirst_blob_at=1s\n144\tsamples=25  nodes=4 walkers=2 rate=30 duration=25s seed=42\n145\tsim_exit=0  (0 = --verify PASSED)\n146\tsim_verify: [SIM] PASS: 2 blobs detected for 2 walkers\n147\treject_in_sim_log=0\n148\tfps=Average FPS: ~360\n149\t```\n150\t\n151\t---\n152\t\n153\t## Acceptance — bf-40hc (independently re-verified 2026-07-08, HEAD `f859f3e`)\n154\t\n155\t`./scripts/run-sim-local.sh` is the bf-40hc deliverable. Re-running it at `f859f3e`\n156\tproduced `exit 0` with this evidence, mapping each acceptance criterion:\n157\t\n158\t| bf-40hc acceptance criterion | Verified evidence (2026-07-08, HEAD `f859f3e`) |\n159\t|------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|\n160\t| Documented command starts the mothership without a runtime panic/crash | `[run-sim] mothership healthy` after the `/healthz` poll; clean startup, no panic — mothership served the full 25 s run and shut down via the trap |\n161\t| At least one blob object observable at runtime (API / WebSocket / tracker log) | Independent `/api/blobs` poller: `peak_blob_count=2`, `first_blob_at=1s` (nodes_online=4 throughout); `sim --verify` secondary cross-check: `[SIM] PASS: 2 blobs detected for 2 walkers` |\n162\t| Reproduce command written into the bead body or a referenced note | This file + `scripts/run-sim-local.sh`; exact flags: `--nodes 4 --walkers 2 --rate 30 --space 5x5x2.5 --duration 25 --seed 42 --verify` |\n163\t\n164\tReference run captured: `peak=2  first_blob=1s  sim_exit=0  verify=PASS  rejects=0  fps≈363`.\n165\t\n166\t**Out of scope — do not confuse with bf-40hc:** the `tests/e2e` tests\n167\t`TestDetectionEvents`, `TestFullE2EIntegration`, and `TestConcurrentNodes` are\n168\t**deliberately RED by design** (see their inline NOTE comments) — they are gated on\n169\tthe upstream fusion `SetNodePosition` wiring (**bf-4q5w**) and are the strictness\n170\tcontract of the **bf-5jeo** verification capstone. `TestConcurrentNodes` additionally\n171\tconnects raw tokenless nodes against the harness's `SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0`\n172\tstrict mode, so its rejects are a harness artifact, not a pipeline defect. They must\n173\tNOT be weakened to turn green, and they are not a bf-40hc regression: bf-40hc asserts\n174\tonly that *a* hardware-free runtime path emits a tracked blob, which `run-sim-local.sh`\n175\tdeterministically proves.\n176\t\n177\t---\n178\t\n179\t## For later beads (bf-f841 and beyond)\n180\t\n181\t- **Reuse, don't re-derive.** Start from `scripts/run-sim-local.sh`. If you need a\n182\t  runtime assertion, run the script (or import its param block) rather than\n183\t  hand-rolling a `curl /api/blobs`.\n184\t- **Determinism contract:** `--seed` pins walker paths; `--rate 30` pins blob\n185\t  emission. The only residual nondeterminism is OS scheduling jitter, which the\n186\t  peak-over-window poller absorbs.\n187\t- **Adding identity checks (bf-f841):** the sim emits synthetic BLE advertisements\n188\t  with `--ble`; pair `--ble` with the BLE-registry REST flow. The token/CSI/blob\n189\t  path this script exercises is the prerequisite — keep it green.\n190\t- **Predecessor:** `blob_observation.sh` (repo root) is the bf-51fq-specific\n191\t  harness with the same logic and detailed inline comments; this script is the\n192\t  bead-agnostic canonical form that supersedes it for general use.\n193\t\n194\t## Keeping this honest\n195\t\n196\tIf a clean run of `scripts/run-sim-local.sh` ever fails to reach `peak ≥ 1`:\n197\t1. Rebuild both binaries (`rm /tmp/spaxel-sim /tmp/spaxel-mothership` and rerun).\n200\t3. Try `SIM_RATE=35` to rule out a jitter-induced threshold miss.\n201\t4. Only then treat it as a real defect and update this note + the script defaults.\n202\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e3075ab7-0986-4e8e-962e-70392aecf683","uuid":"90c4eb76-d09c-49a8-8777-167ca5629039","timestamp":"2026-07-08T12:37:03.444Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/spaxel/notes/hardware-free-runtime.md","content":"# Hardware-Free Runtime Reproduce\n\n**Canonical script:** `scripts/run-sim-local.sh`\n**Verified:** 2026-07-08 — `5bbe52c` (post bf-243os sim→blob path fix), re-verified at HEAD `f859f3e` for bf-40hc acceptance, and final-clean-rebuild re-verified at HEAD `c927f67` (the bf-40hc doc commit itself).\n**Resolves:** the ambiguity that caused bf-40hc's repeated failures; closes the chain bf-3zll → bf-3hji → bf-51fq → bf-5cr3, and **satisfies bf-40hc** (hardware-free runtime path that creates a blob).\n\nThis is the single deterministic artifact that captures the working end-to-end\nruntime path — **sim connects → CSI ingested → phase/fusion → tracked blob** — with\nzero hardware, zero Docker, zero manual IP, and zero manual token. Later beads\n(e.g. the blob-identity runtime checks under **bf-f841**) and reruns should start\nfrom here instead of re-deriving the command sequence or the tuning.\n\n---\n\n## TL;DR\n\n```bash\n./scripts/run-sim-local.sh\n# expects, within ~30s:\n#   [run-sim] mothership healthy\n#     t= 1s  nodes_online=4  blobs=2  peak=2\n#     ...\n#   [run-sim] peak_blob_count=2\n#   [run-sim] PASS: >=1 blob via /api/blobs (peak=2) AND sim --verify passed\n```\n\nExit 0 iff ≥1 tracked blob is observed via `GET /api/blobs` over the run window.\n\n---\n\n## What the script does (the exact reproduce sequence)\n\n1. **Build mothership from source** — `cd mothership && go build -o /tmp/spaxel-mothership ./cmd/mothership`\n2. **Build the canonical sim from source** — `cd mothership && go build -o /tmp/spaxel-sim ./cmd/sim`\n3. **Start the mothership** on an ephemeral port + data dir:\n   ```bash\n   SPAXEL_BIND_ADDR=127.0.0.1:8088 \\\n   SPAXEL_DATA_DIR=$(mktemp -d) \\\n   SPAXEL_LOG_LEVEL=warn \\\n   SPAXEL_MDNS_ENABLED=false \\\n   TZ=UTC \\\n     /tmp/spaxel-mothership &\n   ```\n4. **Wait for health** — poll `GET /healthz` until `{\"status\":\"ok\"}`.\n5. **Run the sim** with `--verify`:\n   ```bash\n   /tmp/spaxel-sim \\\n     --mothership ws://localhost:8088/ws/node \\\n     --nodes 4 --walkers 2 --rate 30 --space 5x5x2.5 \\\n     --duration 25 --seed 42 --verify\n   ```\n6. **Observe** — poll `GET /api/blobs` once per second for the whole streaming\n   window, keep the peak, gate on `peak ≥ 1`. (`sim --verify` is a reported\n   secondary cross-check that asserts `blob_count == walkers ±1`.)\n\nEach step is in `scripts/run-sim-local.sh` with a `trap` cleanup that kills both\nprocesses and removes the temp data dir.\n\n---\n\n## The two gotchas (read these before touching the path)\n\n### 1. There are TWO `cmd/sim` packages — only one has `--verify`\n\n| Path | Module | Has `--verify`? | Use it? |\n|------|--------|-----------------|---------|\n| `mothership/cmd/sim/` | part of the mothership module | **YES** (`verify.go`) | **YES — canonical** |\n| `cmd/sim/` | separate, older module (own `go.mod`) | no | **NO** |\n\n`scripts/run-sim-local.sh` builds **only** from `mothership/cmd/sim` and\nadditionally guards against a stale binary by checking `--help` for `--verify`,\nrebuilding if missing. **Do not** build/run `cmd/sim` for runtime blob checks.\n\n### 2. `rate=30`, not the upstream default `20`\n\nAt the upstream default `--rate 20`, fusion produces a peak only *while* a walker\nmoves enough to cross the DeltaRMS threshold, so a run can legitimately land\n`peak=2` *or* `peak=0` nondeterministically (see the comment in\n`mothership/cmd/sim/verify.go`). Bumping `--rate` to **30** reliably clears the\nthreshold: every observed run hits `peak == walkers` with the first blob at ~1s.\nThis is a rate/tuning fact, **not** a broken pipeline — if a bead reports 0 blobs,\nfirst re-run at `rate=30` before suspecting a defect.\n\n`--duration 25` (not 40) keeps the sim's late-window `--verify` sample inside the\nactive-walk phase; at long durations walkers drift into low-signal positions and\nverify can spuriously report 0 even though the `/api/blobs` window still saw blobs.\n\n---\n\n## Token path (no manual token needed)\n\nThe canonical sim authenticates exactly like real provisioned firmware:\n\n- `--token` defaults to empty → the sim calls `POST /api/provision` to mint a real\n  per-node HMAC token (`HMAC-SHA256(install_secret, node_mac)`), sent as the\n  `X-Spaxel-Token` header on the WebSocket dial, then bridged into `hello.Token`\n  for validation (bf-1o7qi).\n- Fallback: if provisioning fails, the sim generates a synthetic token. Either\n  way the connection is not left tokenless-by-design.\n- Defense-in-depth: the mothership also tolerates tokenless nodes for\n  `SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS` (default 24h) after startup, so even a token\n  glitch during a reproduce run does not cause a `reject` (bf-2hdbg). The script\n  counts `reject`/`401`/`403` lines in the sim log and reports the count; expect 0.\n\nIf you ever see rejects, check `SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS` (the e2e harness\nsets it to `0` to force strict validation) and that the binary is built from\n`mothership/cmd/sim`.\n\n---\n\n## Environment (mothership)\n\n| Variable | Value in the script | Why |\n|----------|---------------------|-----|\n| `SPAXEL_BIND_ADDR` | `127.0.0.1:8088` | Loopback; avoid clashing with anything on 8080 |\n| `SPAXEL_DATA_DIR` | ephemeral `mktemp -d` | Clean state every run; removed on exit |\n| `SPAXEL_LOG_LEVEL` | `warn` | Quiet enough to scan, loud enough on real problems |\n| `SPAXEL_MDNS_ENABLED` | `false` | Loopback reproduce needs no multicast; sim dials directly |\n| `TZ` | `UTC` | Deterministic diurnal/timestamp behavior |\n\n## Parameters (sim)\n\n| Flag | Default in script | Notes |\n|------|-------------------|-------|\n| `--nodes` | `4` | Enough for stable multi-link fusion |\n| `--walkers` | `2` | `peak == walkers` at rate=30 |\n| `--rate` | `30` | **See gotcha #2** — 20 is nondeterministic |\n| `--duration` | `25` | Keeps `--verify`'s late sample in the active-walk phase |\n| `--seed` | `42` | Reproducible walker paths |\n| `--space` | `5x5x2.5` | Default room geometry |\n| `--verify` | (set) | Canonical acceptance: `blob_count == walkers ±1` + within-2m check |\n\nAll overridable via env: `SIM_NODES`, `SIM_WALKERS`, `SIM_RATE`, `SIM_DURATION`,\n`SIM_SEED`, `SIM_SPACE`, `SIM_PORT`. Binary paths via `SPAXEL_MOTHERSHIP_BIN` /\n`SPAXEL_SIM_BIN`.\n\n---\n\n## Expected result (reference run, HEAD `5bbe52c`)\n\n```\npeak_blob_count=2\nfirst_blob_at=1s\nsamples=25  nodes=4 walkers=2 rate=30 duration=25s seed=42\nsim_exit=0  (0 = --verify PASSED)\nsim_verify: [SIM] PASS: 2 blobs detected for 2 walkers\nreject_in_sim_log=0\nfps=Average FPS: ~360\n```\n\n---\n\n## Acceptance — bf-40hc (independently re-verified 2026-07-08, HEAD `f859f3e`)\n\n`./scripts/run-sim-local.sh` is the bf-40hc deliverable. Re-running it at `f859f3e`\nproduced `exit 0` with this evidence, mapping each acceptance criterion:\n\n| bf-40hc acceptance criterion | Verified evidence (2026-07-08, HEAD `f859f3e`) |\n|------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|\n| Documented command starts the mothership without a runtime panic/crash | `[run-sim] mothership healthy` after the `/healthz` poll; clean startup, no panic — mothership served the full 25 s run and shut down via the trap |\n| At least one blob object observable at runtime (API / WebSocket / tracker log) | Independent `/api/blobs` poller: `peak_blob_count=2`, `first_blob_at=1s` (nodes_online=4 throughout); `sim --verify` secondary cross-check: `[SIM] PASS: 2 blobs detected for 2 walkers` |\n| Reproduce command written into the bead body or a referenced note | This file + `scripts/run-sim-local.sh`; exact flags: `--nodes 4 --walkers 2 --rate 30 --space 5x5x2.5 --duration 25 --seed 42 --verify` |\n\nReference run captured: `peak=2  first_blob=1s  sim_exit=0  verify=PASS  rejects=0  fps≈363`.\n\n**Out of scope — do not confuse with bf-40hc:** the `tests/e2e` tests\n`TestDetectionEvents`, `TestFullE2EIntegration`, and `TestConcurrentNodes` are\n**deliberately RED by design** (see their inline NOTE comments) — they are gated on\nthe upstream fusion `SetNodePosition` wiring (**bf-4q5w**) and are the strictness\ncontract of the **bf-5jeo** verification capstone. `TestConcurrentNodes` additionally\nconnects raw tokenless nodes against the harness's `SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0`\nstrict mode, so its rejects are a harness artifact, not a pipeline defect. They must\nNOT be weakened to turn green, and they are not a bf-40hc regression: bf-40hc asserts\nonly that *a* hardware-free runtime path emits a tracked blob, which `run-sim-local.sh`\ndeterministically proves.\n\n---\n\n## For later beads (bf-f841 and beyond)\n\n- **Reuse, don't re-derive.** Start from `scripts/run-sim-local.sh`. If you need a\n  runtime assertion, run the script (or import its param block) rather than\n  hand-rolling a `curl /api/blobs`.\n- **Determinism contract:** `--seed` pins walker paths; `--rate 30` pins blob\n  emission. The only residual nondeterminism is OS scheduling jitter, which the\n  peak-over-window poller absorbs.\n- **Adding identity checks (bf-f841):** the sim emits synthetic BLE advertisements\n  with `--ble`; pair `--ble` with the BLE-registry REST flow. The token/CSI/blob\n  path this script exercises is the prerequisite — keep it green.\n- **Predecessor:** `blob_observation.sh` (repo root) is the bf-51fq-specific\n  harness with the same logic and detailed inline comments; this script is the\n  bead-agnostic canonical form that supersedes it for general use.\n\n## Keeping this honest\n\nIf a clean run of `scripts/run-sim-local.sh` ever fails to reach `peak ≥ 1`:\n1. Confirm HEAD includes the sim→blob fix (the `bf-243os` commit and\n   `b9f362c` LinkID-split fix) — `grep` `fusion.go` for stray debug flags.\n2. Rebuild both binaries (`rm /tmp/spaxel-sim /tmp/spaxel-mothership` and rerun).\n3. Try `SIM_RATE=35` to rule out a jitter-induced threshold miss.\n4. Only then treat it as a real defect and update this note + the script defaults.\n","numLines":202,"startLine":1,"totalLines":202}}}
